prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='svr', seasonality_champion='FT_catboost', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: FT_catboost dominates as overall champion with best RMSE (7175.64), excellent seasonality capture (0.5249), and solid trend tracking. However, high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=1.68) and high seasonality variance (0.61) indicate models capture patterns very differently. The early horizon is best served by ARIMA/FT_catboost while late horizons favor DWT_rf/ONLY_DWT_rf. Given both high disagreement and high seasonal variance, DBA combination is the optimal approach to leverage model diversity while managing disagreement.
prompts.skeptic:
Chame build_debate_packet_tool() PRIMEIRO (inputs via context). Depois retorne APENAS JSON (sem markdown) com add_names, remove_names, params_overrides, rationale, changes, when_good.
IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "svr",
  "seasonality_champion": "FT_catboost",
  "overall_champion": "FT_catboost",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "ARIMA", "late": "DWT_rf"},
  "tier1_models": ["FT_catboost", "ONLY_DWT_rf", "DWT_rf", "ONLY_DWT_svr", "FT_rf", "CWT_catboost", "ONLY_DWT_catboost", "FT_svr"],
  "tier2_models": ["svr", "ONLY_FT_rf", "THETA", "DWT_catboost", "NaiveMovingAverage", "ETS", "ARIMA", "ONLY_FT_svr"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 1.6782,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["up", "up", "down"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "FT_catboost dominates as overall champion with best RMSE (7175.64), excellent seasonality capture (0.5249), and solid trend tracking. However, high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=1.68) and high seasonality variance (0.61) indicate models capture patterns very differently. The early horizon is best served by ARIMA/FT_catboost while late horizons favor DWT_rf/ONLY_DWT_rf. Given both high disagreement and high seasonal variance, DBA combination is the optimal approach to leverage model diversity while managing disagreement."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "best_per_horizon_by_validation", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k3", "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35"],
  "params_overrides": {"topk_mean_per_horizon_k3": {"top_k": 3}, "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35": {"top_k": 3, "shrinkage": 0.35}},
  "score_preset": "rmse_focus",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=5 indicates per-horizon selection is appropriate. relative_spread_mean=0.347 (>0.25) signals high model disagreement favoring DBA combination (pattern_analyst_insights recommended_method_hint). RMSE spread ratio=1.68 suggests top-k with small k and shrinkage. n_windows=3 requires conservative top_k=3 and shrinkage=0.35. FT_catboost dominates as overall champion but diversity is needed given high disagreement."
}
raw.skeptic: {"add_names": [], "remove_names": [], "params_overrides": {}, "rationale": "Current set has 4 candidates with 3 different types (selection, weighted, baseline) - good diversity. All candidates are from valid_candidate_names. n_windows=3 is low, so current selection/weighted strategies using anti-leakage rolling/expanding mode are safe. No redundancy detected. Current top candidates have scores >0.76 which outperform universe ranking_top candidates (~0.74-0.75). No changes needed.", "changes": [], "when_good": ""}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: FT_catboost dominates as overall champion with best RMSE (7175.64), excellent seasonality capture (0.5249), and solid trend tracking. However, high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=1.68) and high seasonality variance (0.61) indicate models capture patterns very differently. The early horizon is best served by ARIMA/FT_catboost while late horizons favor DWT_rf/ONLY_DWT_rf. Given both high disagreement and high seasonal variance, DBA combination is the optimal approach to leverage model diversity while managing disagreement.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=5 indicates per-horizon selection is appropriate. relative_spread_mean=0.347 (>0.25) signals high model disagreement favoring DBA combination (pattern_analyst_insights recommended_method_hint). RMSE spread ratio=1.68 suggests top-k with small k and shrinkage. n_windows=3 requires conservative top_k=3 and shrinkage=0.35. FT_catboost dominates as overall champion but diversity is needed given high disagreement.

skeptic.rationale: Current set has 4 candidates with 3 different types (selection, weighted, baseline) - good diversity. All candidates are from valid_candidate_names. n_windows=3 is low, so current selection/weighted strategies using anti-leakage rolling/expanding mode are safe. No redundancy detected. Current top candidates have scores >0.76 which outperform universe ranking_top candidates (~0.74-0.75). No changes needed.
